description: Push 3 of the retention lifecycle: everything fixed, as an open-PR re-review snapshot. Every prior thread (the two kept blocking threads and the two push-2 findings) is addressed, so the reconciler must resolve all four and the verdict must flip to APPROVE. The deliberately fire-and-forget prune (rejections swallowed via .catch, documented as intentional) is a trap: it must not be flagged. Until 2026-07-20 the prune was a bare `void` call, a real crash-on-rejection defect the reviewer correctly blocked in every drift sample; the tree now implements the documented intent so the expected APPROVE is fair.

prContext.title: notes: cap stored notes per user and delete them with user data
prContext.description: Adds a per-user retention cap enforced on save, and wires note deletion into account deletion. Push 3: addresses all review feedback.
prContext.author: dev-notes

priorThreads.body: **issue (blocking):** Off-by-one - with newest-first ordering, `offset: MAX_NOTES_PER_USER - 1` keeps one note fewer than the cap. Use `offset: MAX_NOTES_PER_USER`.
priorThreads.authorReply: Sorry, overcorrected last push - now offset: MAX_NOTES_PER_USER exactly.

priorThreads.body: **todo (blocking):** The prune test saves 2 notes against a cap of 200 and asserts only that the promise resolves; it can never exercise the cap. Save past the cap and assert the retained count.
priorThreads.authorReply: Added a real cap test that saves past the cap and asserts the count.

priorThreads.body: **issue (blocking):** Dedup keys on a lowercased 8-character prefix, so distinct notes sharing a prefix collide and the later one is silently dropped - data loss with no error.
priorThreads.authorReply: Now compares full content inside the window; added a shared-prefix test.

priorThreads.body: **suggestion (non-blocking):** The new 30-day dedup window has no test on either side of the boundary; a regression in the window filter would pass CI silently.
priorThreads.authorReply: Covered by the new dedup tests.
